name: Multifocal Atrial Tachycardia
identifyBy: Irregular rhythm with DISTINCT P waves of ≥3 different morphologies
Rate >100 bpm (if <100 bpm = wandering atrial pacemaker)
Variable PR intervals (each focus has different conduction time)
No dominant atrial pacemaker - multiple competing foci
Classic context: severe COPD, hypoxia, hypomagnesaemia, heart failure

measurements.rhythm: Irregular (but has P waves before each QRS)

management: Treat the underlying cause - MAT rarely responds to antiarrhythmics alone
COPD: optimise bronchodilators, treat infection, correct hypoxia
Correct electrolytes: Magnesium 2g IV (even if normal levels - effective)
Verapamil may slow rate (but check lung function - some bronchospasm risk)
Avoid digoxin - usually ineffective for MAT
Note: cardioversion is NOT effective for MAT (not a re-entrant rhythm)
pearls: MAT is pathognomonic of COPD with cor pulmonale in the right clinical context
Magnesium is the most effective first-line drug for rate control in MAT
Easily confused with AF - the key difference: MAT has distinct P waves before each QRS
Theophylline toxicity is a classic cause - check drug history
Cardioversion is completely ineffective - MAT is an automatic (not re-entrant) tachycardia
differentials: AF (no distinct P waves, no isoelectric baseline between P waves)

qaNotes: Supraventricular rhythm migrated from v1 components; verify P-wave relationship in editor.
